title: HTML page structure
summary: Learn the basic structure that holds a normal HTML page together.
goal: By the end of this lesson, you will be able to recognize and write a simple HTML page structure.
explanation: HTML uses tags to describe the parts of a webpage. Tags tell the browser what each piece of content is.
A basic HTML page usually has a doctype, an html element, a head section, and a body section.
The head stores information about the page, while the body stores the content the user sees.
code:
Welcome to Coding Hub

This is a simple HTML page.
codeNotes: <!DOCTYPE html> tells the browser to use modern HTML rules.
<head> contains information about the page, such as the title.
<body> contains the content users actually see, like headings and paragraphs.
commonMistakes: Putting visible content inside the head instead of the body.

Forgetting a closing tag like

or .
Misspelling common tags such as <title> or <meta>.

exercise.title: Build your first page shell
exercise.instructions: Create a page with a title in the head and a heading plus one paragraph in the body.

title: HTML links and images
summary: Learn how to add clickable links and images to a webpage.
goal: By the end of this lesson, you will be able to add a basic link and image in HTML.
explanation: Links help users move from one page to another. In HTML, links use the <a> tag.
Images display pictures on a page. In HTML, images use the tag.
Attributes give extra information to a tag. For example, href tells a link where to go, while src tells an image where the file lives.
code:
Learning Resources

Visit MDN Web Docs
codeNotes: The <a> tag creates a clickable link.
href is the destination of the link.
target='_blank' opens the link in a new tab.
The tag displays an image, and alt describes it for accessibility and screen readers.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the href attribute in a link.
Leaving out the alt text on an image.
Using an image source that does not exist or is typed incorrectly.

exercise.title: Create a resource section
exercise.instructions: Add one heading, one link to a learning site, and one image with meaningful alt text.
